name: anchor-strong
description: A strong profile: four artifact types, rich evidence, quantified achievements, clear narrative, multi-surface presence
_notes: 4 artifacts with 2 summaries each. Expected scores ~50 per dim after sufficiency penalty (2/6 artifact types missing).

artifacts.content:
# Alex Chen
Principal Engineer at HyperScale (2019-2024)
Architected distributed caching layer serving 50M requests/day, cutting P99 latency from 2s to 180ms.
Led 8-engineer team through 3 major feature launches.
Open source contributor: 400+ GitHub stars on distributed-cache-lib.
Education: MS Computer Science, Stanford.

# Alex Chen — LinkedIn
Principal Engineer | HyperScale | Distributed Systems
About: I build systems that scale. 10+ years in distributed systems, Kubernetes, Go, Python.
Featured: Blog post on distributed caching (2,000 views), Open Source project.
Experience: 3 leadership roles, quantified outcomes in each.
Skills: Go, Python, Kubernetes, Distributed Systems, System Design.

# Alex Chen — GitHub
distributed-cache-lib: 400 stars, actively maintained. go-perf-toolkit: 120 stars.
Pinned repos show system design focus. README files with architecture diagrams.
15 public repositories, regular commit cadence.

# alexchen.dev
Personal site: case studies for HyperScale caching project and distributed-cache-lib.
Blog: 6 posts on distributed systems, Kubernetes, and Go performance.
Speaking: 2 conference talks listed with slides.
Clear positioning as distributed systems expert.

evidence_summaries.content: Name: Alex Chen, Role: Principal Engineer, Years: 10+, Skills: Go, Python, Kubernetes, Distributed Systems
Architected caching layer 50M req/day, P99 2s→180ms. Led 8-engineer team. Quantified outcomes on all major accomplishments.
LinkedIn complete: headline, about, featured, experience with metrics, skills, recommendations present.
Strong narrative: distributed systems expert framing consistent across headline, about, and featured. Cross-surface alignment with resume.
15 public repos, 400+ stars on flagship project. Active commit cadence. Architecture-focused READMEs.
Proof of systems work: caching library with production-grade code quality, go-perf-toolkit for observability.
Personal site with case studies, blog, and speaking history. Consistent distributed systems positioning.
Case studies include architecture diagrams, metrics. Blog has 6 technical posts. Conference talks with slides.
